Title: Deficiency of a triterpene pathway results in humidity-sensitive genic male sterility in rice

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for complementation/overexpression construct Ubi_pro::OsOSC12; methanol-inducible promoter in Pichia pastoris for heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triterpene biosynthesis / poaceatapetol ester pathway in anther and pollen coat formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function of OsOSC12/OsPTS1 creates humidity-sensitive genic male sterility (HGMS), a potentially useful two-line hybrid breeding trait in rice and likely other cereals. Practical deployment would use backcrossing or targeted knockout of PTS1 homologs in dry regions with RH <60% during anthesis; caution that unexpected high humidity or rain can restore fertility and reduce hybrid seed purity. WT allele restores fertility, so trait is genetically tractable.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into the bicyclic triterpene poaceatapetol, initiating a committed triterpene branch whose products are further esterified with fatty acids in the pollen coat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsOSC12 promotes synthesis of poaceatapetol/poaceatapetol esters, enables normal pollen coat formation and accumulation of key fatty acids in the pollen coat, and thereby prevents rapid pollen dehydration. Loss-of-function causes humidity-sensitive genic male sterility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING-derived loss-of-function mutants; genetic analysis and allelism tests; complementation with WT OsOSC12; expression profiling (northern, qRT-PCR, western, in situ, immunolocalization); GC-MS, HR-ESI-MS and NMR metabolite identification; heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; TEM/cryo-TEM; exogenous fatty-acid rescue assays; pollen dehydration/germination assays under different humidity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms pathway product poaceatapetol, which is the immediate triterpene backbone for poaceatapetol esters and indirectly enables accumulation of fatty acids needed for the functional pollen coat protecting pollen hydration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Relative humidity is decisive: mutants are male sterile at low/ambient RH (<60%) and fertile at high RH (>80%); assays also done at 25-35°C or field 30-39°C, and authors state HGMS is not driven by temperature or photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anther-specific; strongly expressed in tapetal cells during relatively late anther development stages S8-S11, especially around S9-S10/S11
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Anther-specific; strongly expressed in tapetal cells during relatively late anther development stages S8-S11, especially around S9-S10/S11
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; branch point/early pathway step in a grass-specific triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper renames OsOSC12 as OsPTS1 after demonstrating function. Poaceatapetol esters detected in WT include palmitate (16:0), oleate (18:1), and stearate (18:0) esters. TE fraction alone could not rescue pollen hydration, indicating triterpene products act indirectly, likely by enabling transport/accumulation of fatty acids from tapetum to pollen coat. Homologous transcripts/protein/activity were detected in barley, maize, and wheat anthers, suggesting cereal conservation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Grass-specific poaceatapetol / poaceatapetol ester branch of triterpene metabolism associated with pollen coat biogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function causes severe male sterility under low RH and poor self-seed set; fertility is environmentally unstable because high humidity restores male fertility, which is useful for breeding but a management risk under rainy/high-humidity conditions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a committed-step and branch-point control enzyme for this triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsOSC12 is an anther/tapetum-expressed oxidosqualene cyclase required for normal pollen coat formation. 2. Biochemical and heterologous-expression evidence show it cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to poaceatapetol, defining a new committed triterpene pathway; thus OsOSC12 is poaceatapetol synthase (OsPTS1). 3. Loss-of-function mutants lack pollen coat material, show reduced pollen-coat fatty acids and poaceatapetol esters, rapidly dehydrate at RH <60%, and become humidity-sensitive genic male sterile. 4. High humidity or exogenous mixtures containing linolenic acid plus palmitic acid or stearic acid rescue pollen hydration, indicating OsPTS1 indirectly promotes fatty-acid accumulation needed for pollen desiccation protection. 5. The trait has clear value for HGMS-based hybrid breeding in rice and possibly other grasses.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Poaceatapetol esters / functional pollen-coat lipid composition (notably supported accumulation of palmitic acid, stearic acid, and linolenic acid in pollen coat)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anther/pollen coat of maturing pollen grains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Prevents over-dehydration of released pollen grains, thereby maintaining pollen viability, stigma adhesion, germination, and male fertility under ambient humidity
